description: Gold-standard known cross-domain isomorphisms, each realized as a pair of real arXiv papers from different archives. Curated 2026-07-05 by agent search over the arXiv API; every paper's LaTeX source availability was verified at curation time.

pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are linear second-order parabolic PDEs; the Black-Scholes PDE transforms via a change of variables into the standard heat/diffusion equation.

side_a.title: On a method of solving the Black-Scholes Equation

side_b.title: Non-steady state heat conduction in composite walls

pairs.analogy_name: Ising model vs Hopfield network / associative memory
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are systems of binary spins/neurons with pairwise couplings whose dynamics minimize a quadratic energy (Lyapunov) function.

side_b.title: Universal Hopfield Networks: A General Framework for Single-Shot Associative Memory Models

pairs.analogy_name: Lotka-Volterra predator-prey vs firm/market competition dynamics
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are coupled nonlinear ODEs with logistic growth and bilinear interaction terms describing competitive/predatory coupling.

side_a.title: Novel predator-prey model admitting exact analytical solution

side_b.title: Measuring inequality in society-oriented Lotka--Volterra-type kinetic equations
side_b.note: replaces 1905.06364 and 1212.1282, whose LaTeX sources were not fetchable; macroscopic dynamics of this paper are an explicit Lotka-Volterra ODE system

pairs.analogy_name: SIR epidemic model vs rumor/information spreading
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both use compartmental S-I-R(S) ODEs where individuals transition between susceptible, infected/spreader, and recovered states via mass-action contact terms.

side_a.title: Confinement strategies in a simple SIR model

side_b.title: A deterministic mathematical model for the spread of two rumors

pairs.analogy_name: Kuramoto coupled-oscillator synchronization vs power-grid dynamics
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are networks of phase oscillators coupled through the sine of phase differences; power-grid swing equations are a second-order (inertial) Kuramoto model.

side_a.title: The Kuramoto model on oriented and signed graphs

side_b.title: Dynamical heterogeneity and universality of power-grids

pairs.analogy_name: Fokker-Planck / Langevin dynamics vs score-based diffusion generative models
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both describe a probability density evolving under an SDE (Langevin) whose density obeys the Fokker-Planck equation; diffusion generative models reverse this SDE using the learned score.

side_a.title: Brownian motion with dry friction: Fokker-Planck approach

side_b.title: Closing the ODE-SDE gap in score-based diffusion models through the Fokker-Planck equation

pairs.analogy_name: Replicator dynamics vs mirror descent / multiplicative weights
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are the same entropic/Bregman gradient flow on the probability simplex: weights updated multiplicatively in proportion to relative fitness or negative gradient.

side_a.title: Stochastic Replicator Dynamics Subject to Markovian Switching

side_b.title: Mirrorless Mirror Descent: A Natural Derivation of Mirror Descent

pairs.analogy_name: Logistic population growth vs technology adoption / innovation diffusion
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are governed by the logistic (Verhulst) ODE: growth proportional to current level times remaining unsaturated capacity.

side_a.title: Stochastic dynamics and logistic population growth

side_b.title: Adoption of Innovations with Contrarians and Repentant Agents

pairs.analogy_name: Damped driven mechanical oscillator vs electronic oscillator circuit
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are second-order oscillator ODEs with damping and driving/feedback terms; the mechanical-electrical analogy maps mass<->inductance, damping<->resistance, stiffness<->inverse capacitance.

side_a.title: Resonance oscillation of a damped driven simple pendulum

side_b.title: Analog cross coupled controller for oscillations: modeling and design via dominance theory
side_b.note: v2 replacement for 2012.11533 (Monotone RLC Circuits), whose core model was operator-theoretic optimization rather than circuit dynamics — a v1 ground-truth curation error documented in results/ANALYSIS-2026-07-05.md

pairs.analogy_name: Burgers equation vs traffic flow (LWR kinematic waves)
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are scalar conservation laws with nonlinear flux producing shocks and kinematic waves.

side_a.title: Self-similar solutions to the hypoviscous Burgers and SQG equations at criticality

side_b.title: Follow-the-Leader models can be viewed as a numerical approximation to the Lighthill-Whitham-Richards model for traffic flow

pairs.analogy_name: Statistical-mechanics free energy / maximum entropy vs variational inference
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both minimize a free energy (energy minus temperature times entropy) / maximize entropy under moment constraints; the Gibbs variational principle is formally identical to variational free-energy inference.

side_a.title: Jarzynski equality in the context of maximum path entropy

side_b.title: Variational Free Energies for Compressed Sensing

pairs.analogy_name: Optimal transport (Monge-Kantorovich) vs economics matching / equilibrium
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both couple two marginal distributions to minimize aggregate cost; Kantorovich duality yields prices/potentials, making stable matching an optimal-transport plan.

side_a.title: A characterization for solutions of the Monge-Kantorovich mass transport problem

side_b.title: Stable Matching as Transport: a Welfarist Perspective on Market Design

pairs.analogy_name: Neuronal cable equation vs Telegrapher's equation
pairs.known_shared_structure: Both are the same one-dimensional damped diffusion/wave PDE for voltage along a distributed RC/RLC line.
pairs.documented_where: Rall (1977) cable theory; Koch, Biophysics of Computation (1999).

side_b.title: Telegraph equations for the case of a waveguide with moving boundary

pairs.analogy_name: Paraxial wave equation (beam optics) vs Schroedinger equation
pairs.known_shared_structure: The paraxial envelope equation for a monochromatic beam is formally identical to the 2D time-dependent Schroedinger equation (propagation coordinate as time, refractive index as potential).

side_b.title: A new approach to solving the Schrodinger equation

pairs.analogy_name: Sine-Gordon: long Josephson junction vs soliton field theory
pairs.known_shared_structure: Long-Josephson-junction phase dynamics obey the sine-Gordon equation, the same nonlinear Klein-Gordon-type field equation studied for kink/breather solitons.
pairs.documented_where: Barone & Paterno (1982); Dauxois & Peyrard, Physics of Solitons (2006).

side_a.title: Sine-Gordon breathers generation in driven long Josephson junctions

side_b.title: Sine-Gordon solitons in networks: Scattering and transmission at vertices

held_out_note: held_out pairs curated 2026-07-05 by an agent not shown the feature vocabulary or schema; reported separately, not part of the pre-registered criterion
